title: Bug V + Z — fab install diff-mode idempotency (planFreshPath → DiffFileState classifier)
scope: packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts + InstallStepResult type + i18n locales + test helpers + new diff-mode test file
description: Refactor planFreshPath (install.ts:1020) from throw-on-existing-unless-force to a non-throwing DiffFileState classifier returning one of: missing | present-canonical | drifted | user-modified. Split state-detection (always runs, never throws) from state-transition (only mutates/throws when actually writing AND drift detected). This unblocks both Bug V (idempotency on existing workspace) and Bug Z (--dry-run on existing workspace) which share root cause: throw fires during buildInitFabricPlan BEFORE executeInitExecutionPlan reaches its planOnly branch. On missing → auto-apply (leverages existing copyTextIdempotent / mergeJsonIdempotent — 80% of write surface is already idempotent). On drifted/user-modified without --force → ABORT with helpful message pointing to `fab doctor` (read) or `fab uninstall && fab install` (reset). On present-canonical → print one-line confirmation 'Workspace already canonical (N files verified)' and exit 0. Existing --force / --reapply flags STAY in rc.14 as legacy escape hatches with deprecation warning (slated for removal in rc.15). Introduce separate DiffFileState type alongside InitWriteAction (translate at rendering boundary). Extend InstallStepResult status enum with new variants (drift, missing-managed). Emit install_diff_applied ledger event for diff-mode runs; reapply_completed stays distinct. Hoist snapshotTree + runInit helpers into init-test-utils.ts. Create new install-diff-mode.test.ts. Fill .cursor snapshot parity gap in idempotency test.

files.target: planFreshPath (L1020-L1030), buildInitFabricPlan (L623-L667), executeInitExecutionPlan (L558-L613), executeInitFabricPlan (L669-L774), preparePlannedPath (L1032-L1037), appendReapplyLedgerEvent (L1326-L1340), resolveInitCliIntent (L477-L501)
files.change: Replace planFreshPath's throw-or-overwrite binary with classifyFreshPath() returning DiffFileState ('missing'|'present-canonical'|'drifted'|'user-modified'). Move throw to a NEW gate inside executeInitExecutionPlan that runs ONLY when not planOnly AND state ∈ {drifted, user-modified} AND !options.force. buildInitFabricPlan becomes non-throwing — runs classification for all 3 scaffold files and produces a plan with DiffFileState attached per path. planOnly branch (L571-L579) renders the diff table from the classification result and exits 0 regardless of state (drift visible in preview but not aborting). On state===present-canonical for all 3 + downstream skills/hooks: print 'Workspace already canonical (N files verified)' and exit 0. On state===missing for any file: auto-apply via existing copyTextIdempotent / mergeJsonIdempotent / atomicWriteJson. Per-file detection rules: agents.meta.json → structural compare (parse + key check; tolerant to user-extended counters but flag schema drift); hook scripts (.cjs) → byte compare (templates verbatim); MCP configs → deep-merge-aware (check fabric block presence and match against template); events.jsonl → presence-only (any existing file is canonical; preserve verbatim); forensic.json → always rewrite (snapshot artifact, not config). Add CLI deprecation warning when --force or --reapply is passed (e.g. 'Note: --force is a legacy escape hatch and will be removed in v2.0.0-rc.15. Use `fab uninstall && fab install` for a clean reset.'). Extend appendReapplyLedgerEvent to emit event_type 'install_diff_applied' for diff-mode runs with payload describing which files were missing-applied vs canonical-verified; keep 'reapply_completed' distinct under --reapply. Add lock-check (checkLockOrThrow) coverage for default install on already-init workspace (currently only fires for --reapply at L312-L316) — needed because diff-mode emits ledger events that open events.jsonl.

files.target: new exported type DiffFileState near planFreshPath
files.change: Add `export type DiffFileState = 'missing' | 'present-canonical' | 'drifted' | 'user-modified'`. Add `formatDiffFileState(state: DiffFileState): string` formatter mirroring formatInitPathAction (L1608) for human-readable preview rendering. Add `classifyFreshPath(path: string, expectedCanonical: ...): DiffFileState` that owns the per-file detection rules. InitWriteAction stays unchanged ('created'|'overwritten') — translation happens at the rendering boundary inside executeInitFabricPlan when actually writing.

files.target: InstallStepResult type definition + copyTextIdempotent (L586-L604) + mergeJsonIdempotent (L606-L620) + addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection (L513-L580) consumers
files.change: Extend InstallStepResult status enum from 'written'|'skipped'|'error' to 'written'|'skipped'|'error'|'drift'|'missing-managed'. Update copyTextIdempotent / mergeJsonIdempotent / addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection to return 'drift' when existing-vs-target differ AND options.detectDrift === true (new flag passed from install.ts diff-mode entry). 'missing-managed' status indicates a managed file was deleted by user — auto-reapply (same as 'written'). Existing 'skipped' semantics preserved for canonical-present case.

Keep cli.install.errors.abort-existing for the --force/--reapply path. Add new keys: (a) cli.install.diff.canonical: 'Workspace already canonical ({count} files verified).', (b) cli.install.diff.applying-missing: 'Applying {count} missing pieces: {files}', (c) cli.install.diff.drift-abort: 'Drift detected in {path}. Run `fab uninstall && fab install` to reset, or `fab doctor` to inspect.', (d) cli.install.diff.deprecation-force: 'Note: --force is a legacy escape hatch and will be removed in v2.0.0-rc.15. Use `fab uninstall && fab install` for a clean reset.', (e) cli.install.diff.deprecation-reapply: 'Note: --reapply is a legacy escape hatch and will be removed in v2.0.0-rc.15. Drift detection runs automatically on every `fab install`.'

files.target: mirror new keys added to en.ts
files.change: Add zh-CN translations for cli.install.diff.canonical | applying-missing | drift-abort | deprecation-force | deprecation-reapply (atomic lockstep with en.ts to avoid i18n gate failures).

Hoist `snapshotTree(rootDir: string, includes: string[]): Record<string, string>` (currently duplicated in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L228-L243 and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts). Hoist `runInit(args: Partial<InitOptions>): Promise<InitResult>` (currently duplicated). Add new helper `seedDriftedFile(rootDir: string, relPath: string, modifier: (orig: string) => string)` for diff-mode tests to byte-mutate a canonical file. Add `seedMissingFile(rootDir: string, relPath: string)` to delete a canonical file. Update install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts imports to consume hoisted versions (remove local copies).

Create new test file with 5 scenarios as describe() blocks: (1) 'canonical no-op': run runInit twice; second run prints 'Workspace already canonical' and exits 0; snapshotTree byte-identical before/after second run. (2) 'missing file auto-applies': run runInit then seedMissingFile('.cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs') then re-run runInit; assert the missing file is restored and summary lists it as applied. (3) 'drift aborts with helpful message': run runInit then seedDriftedFile('.cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs', s => s + '// user edit') then re-run runInit (no flags); assert it THROWS with message containing 'Drift detected' AND 'fab doctor' AND 'fab uninstall && fab install'. (4) '--dry-run on existing workspace works': run runInit then re-run with planOnly=true; assert no throw, output includes per-file DiffFileState classification (canonical/drifted/missing visible). (5) '--force bypasses drift check (legacy path)': seed drift then run with options.force=true; assert overwrite succeeds AND deprecation warning emitted to stderr. Use createWerewolfFixtureRoot + hoisted snapshotTree/runInit/seedDriftedFile/seedMissingFile from init-test-utils.ts.

Update imports to consume hoisted snapshotTree + runInit from init-test-utils.ts (remove local copies). Fill .cursor snapshot parity gap: extend snapshotTree includes from ['.claude', '.codex'] to ['.claude', '.codex', '.cursor'] so cursor-side idempotency is symmetrically enforced.
Update imports to consume hoisted snapshotTree + runInit from init-test-utils.ts (remove local copies). Optionally extend .cursor snapshot symmetry where uninstall round-trip tests use snapshotTree.

files.target: I2 'rejects an already-initialized project when --force is not set' (L33-L40) + I3 reapply idempotency (L77-L115)
files.change: Invert I2: re-run with no flags on already-initialized canonical workspace should SUCCEED (not throw) AND emit 'install_diff_applied' or 'workspace canonical' message. Replace the throw assertion with a new test: 'rejects when canonical file has byte-drift' that pre-seeds drift then asserts throw includes 'Drift detected' / 'fab doctor' / 'fab uninstall'. Repurpose I3 reapply-idempotency tests as default-install-idempotency tests (drop {reapply:true, force:true} options object); add separate it() block preserving legacy --reapply behavior with deprecation warning assertion.

files.target: L46-L61 reapply pairing semantics + L89-L132 reapply over existing init
files.change: Update --reapply test to assert deprecation warning emitted in stderr/log output. Keep plan.options.reapply === true assertion but loosen plan.options.force assertion if force coupling is decoupled (still tightly coupled in rc.14 per resolved clarification; both flags stay). Add new it() block: 'default install on existing canonical workspace is a no-op success' — drives installCommand without flags, asserts result includes 'Workspace already canonical' and 0 errors.

AUDIT — if InstallStepResult.skipped now includes 'drift' / 'missing-managed' status alongside 'skipped' on re-run, the filter+length===3 assertion may break. Adjust filter predicate to be status-aware: filter where status === 'skipped' AND path includes <hook-script-name>. Triple count semantics preserved.

implementation: Read packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts fully (1665 LOC) to map all 30+ call sites of planFreshPath / preparePlannedPath / shouldReplaceWritableDirectory; identify the buildInitFabricPlan → executeInitExecutionPlan boundary.
Add new type `export type DiffFileState = 'missing'|'present-canonical'|'drifted'|'user-modified'` near planFreshPath. Add formatDiffFileState helper mirroring formatInitPathAction.

Refactor planFreshPath into classifyFreshPath (non-throwing) — returns DiffFileState by running per-file detection rule (byte-compare for hook scripts, structural for agents.meta.json, presence-only for events.jsonl, always-canonical for forensic.json which is always rewritten).

Refactor buildInitFabricPlan (L623-L667) — replace 3× planFreshPath calls at L641-L643 with 3× classifyFreshPath calls; produce InitScaffoldPlan with DiffFileState attached per path; NO throws during planning.

Refactor executeInitExecutionPlan (L558-L613) — planOnly branch (L571-L579) renders preview from DiffFileState classifications and exits 0 regardless of state. Mutation branch adds a NEW gate: if any path is drifted/user-modified AND !options.force AND !options.reapply, throw the new i18n drift-abort message (cli.install.diff.drift-abort).

Refactor executeInitFabricPlan (L669-L774) — when state===missing for a file, auto-apply (no force needed). When state===present-canonical, skip with idempotency log line. When state===drifted under --force, overwrite (existing behavior). Translate DiffFileState → InitWriteAction at the write boundary (drift+force → 'overwritten'; missing → 'created'; canonical → 'skipped').
Update resolveInitCliIntent (L477-L501) — keep reapply→force coupling (per resolved clarification, both stay through rc.14). Add deprecation warning emission point.

Update appendReapplyLedgerEvent (L1326-L1340) — extend to emit 'install_diff_applied' event_type when no --reapply but classification ran AND state≠all-canonical; keep 'reapply_completed' distinct under --reapply.

Expand lock-check (checkLockOrThrow) coverage from --reapply-only (L312-L316) to ALL install runs on already-initialized workspaces (presence of .fabric/events.jsonl signals init). Default install now writes ledger events.

Update InstallStepResult type in skills-and-hooks.ts — extend status enum with 'drift'|'missing-managed'. Update copyTextIdempotent / mergeJsonIdempotent / addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection to return 'drift' when detectDrift option is set + content differs.
Add 5 new i18n keys to en.ts AND zh-CN.ts atomically (i18n drift gate fails on async).
Hoist snapshotTree + runInit helpers into __tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts; add seedDriftedFile + seedMissingFile helpers.
Update install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts to import hoisted helpers (remove local copies); fill .cursor snapshot parity gap in idempotency test.
Create new __tests__/integration/install-diff-mode.test.ts with 5 scenarios (canonical noop / missing auto-apply / drift abort / --dry-run on existing / --force legacy bypass).
Rewrite init-guard.test.ts I2 (invert assertion) and I3 (repurpose as default-install-idempotency); preserve a single legacy --reapply test with deprecation warning assertion.
Update install-cli-surface.test.ts L46-L61 to assert deprecation warning on --reapply / --force; add new no-flag-canonical-noop it() block.
Audit hooks-install-validate.test.ts L114-L131 — adjust filter predicate to be status-aware if new enum variants surface in re-run assertions.

Update cli-surface.test.ts.snap via `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u` AND update docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 per DRIFT_HINT (if any new CLI flag surface emerges — likely none in rc.14, but snapshot may shift on help text changes).

Run full test suite: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test:typecheck && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli lint && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test:coverage. Verify coverage thresholds (70/70) still met with new diff-mode source files.
Manual smoke test: in 4 scenarios (canonical / missing one hook / drifted hook / --dry-run on existing) — run `fab install` and visually verify message + exit code + ledger event payload.
Stage all modified files + commit with message: 'fix(rc14): fab install idempotency via diff mode — drift→abort, dry-run works on existing workspace'.

reference.pattern: Three existing idempotency patterns to mirror at scaffold stage: (1) byte-compare-then-skip (copyTextIdempotent L586-L604 — hook scripts) (2) deep-merge-then-jsonEqual (mergeJsonIdempotent L606-L620 — MCP configs) (3) managed-region byte-compare (addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection L513-L580 — CLAUDE.md markers). Diff-mode classification is the missing 4th pattern for .fabric/{agents.meta.json,events.jsonl,forensic.json} scaffold-stage files.
reference.examples: copyTextIdempotent shows the byte-compare semantics; mergeJsonIdempotent shows the deep-merge-equality semantics; init-guard.test.ts I3 already encodes byte-identical-across-reruns assertions that diff-mode generalizes to default install.

criteria: `fab install` on a canonical workspace prints 'Workspace already canonical (N files verified)' (where N = total managed files count) and exits 0
`fab install --dry-run` (or --plan) on a canonical workspace prints a diff table with every row showing DiffFileState===present-canonical, exits 0, writes no files
`fab install --dry-run` on a workspace missing one hook file shows that file as DiffFileState===missing in the diff table, exits 0, writes no files
`fab install` (no flags) on a workspace missing one hook file auto-applies the missing file, prints summary listing the applied path, exits 0

`fab install` (no flags) on a workspace with a byte-modified hook file ABORTS with stderr message containing 'Drift detected', the offending path, AND both remediation suggestions 'fab doctor' AND 'fab uninstall && fab install'
`fab install --force` on a drifted workspace still succeeds (legacy path), overwrites the drifted file, emits deprecation warning 'Note: --force is a legacy escape hatch...' to stderr

`fab install --reapply` on a drifted workspace still succeeds (legacy path), emits deprecation warning 'Note: --reapply is a legacy escape hatch...' to stderr, AND emits ledger event_type='reapply_completed' (distinct from install_diff_applied)
Default `fab install` (no flags) on a workspace with state≠all-canonical emits ledger event_type='install_diff_applied' with payload describing applied/canonical/drifted breakdown
New file packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-diff-mode.test.ts contains 5 it() blocks covering all 5 scenarios; pnpm test passes all 5

snapshotTree + runInit hoisted into packages/cli/__tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts; install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts import from there (zero local duplicates)
.cursor parity gap filled: idempotency test snapshotTree includes ['.claude', '.codex', '.cursor'] (was only first two)
init-guard.test.ts I2 inverted (no-throw on canonical re-run) AND new test added asserting throw on byte-drift
InstallStepResult status enum extended with 'drift' and 'missing-managed' variants; all consumers updated to handle new variants
i18n keys cli.install.diff.{canonical,applying-missing,drift-abort,deprecation-force,deprecation-reapply} present in BOTH en.ts and zh-CN.ts (i18n drift gate passes)
Lock-check (checkLockOrThrow) now fires for default `fab install` on existing-workspace (not just --reapply); test confirms lock conflict detection
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test passes 100%
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test:coverage maintains lines/statements ≥70% threshold (no coverage regression)
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test:typecheck passes (tsc 0 errors)
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli lint passes (knip + eslint clean)
Git commit created with subject 'fix(rc14): fab install idempotency via diff mode — drift→abort, dry-run works on existing workspace'

rationale.chosen_approach: Classifier-not-throw refactor with separate DiffFileState type translated at rendering boundary. Existing --force/--reapply stay as legacy escape hatches for one release (rc.14), removed in rc.15. Drift→abort (no --force-override path in default flow). Auto-apply missing pieces leveraging existing copyTextIdempotent/mergeJsonIdempotent (80% of write surface already idempotent).
alternatives_considered: Extend InitWriteAction enum directly with new variants — rejected per resolved clarification: clean separation via separate DiffFileState type is cleaner, no break in formatInitPathAction switch
Auto-merge drifted files preserving user counter values — rejected: too lenient, hides bugs, conflicts with 'drift→abort' design principle
Replace InitWriteAction entirely with DiffFileState — rejected: breaking change, requires snapshot updates across uninstall + plan-render
Kill --force/--reapply in rc.14 immediately — rejected: kept as legacy escape hatch one more release for safer rollout
decision_factors: Bug V + Bug Z share root cause (single fix unblocks both)
80% of write surface already idempotent (refactor is contained to scaffold stage)
Test surface already supports the pattern (copyTextIdempotent reference)
Resolved clarifications eliminated remaining design ambiguity (separate type, drift→abort, one-line confirmation, --force/--reapply stay, run-actual-init test strategy)
rationale.tradeoffs: Existing tests asserting 'throw on re-run without --force' need rewriting (init-guard.test.ts I2). Existing tests with exact-count assertions on InstallStepResult.skipped may need filter predicates updated for new status variants. Coverage thresholds (70/70) must hold with new source files — diff-mode classifier needs its own dedicated tests in install-diff-mode.test.ts to avoid threshold regression.

manual_checks: Run `fab install` in fresh tmpdir (no .fabric) — verify normal fresh-install path still works, all files created
Re-run `fab install` in same tmpdir — verify 'Workspace already canonical' message + exit 0 + zero file writes
Delete .cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs then re-run `fab install` — verify file restored, summary lists it as applied, exit 0
Hand-edit .cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs (append a comment) then re-run `fab install` — verify abort with 'Drift detected' message pointing to fab doctor + uninstall, exit non-zero
Hand-edit drifted file then run `fab install --force` — verify overwrite succeeds + deprecation warning in stderr
Hand-edit drifted file then run `fab install --reapply` — verify overwrite succeeds + DIFFERENT deprecation warning (reapply-specific) in stderr + reapply_completed ledger event
Run `fab install --dry-run` on canonical workspace — verify diff table renders, all rows show canonical, exit 0, no writes
Run `fab install --dry-run` on workspace with one missing hook — verify diff table renders, missing row visible, exit 0, no writes
Inspect .fabric/events.jsonl after diff-mode run — verify install_diff_applied event with payload describing the applied/canonical breakdown
Start `fab serve` then attempt `fab install` (default flow, no flags) — verify lock-check fires (currently only fires under --reapply)
success_metrics: 0 throws during buildInitFabricPlan classification (all state detection runs without error)
Coverage of new classifyFreshPath + formatDiffFileState functions ≥80% (well above 70% threshold)
0 'planFreshPath throws on existing path' references remain in tests except 1 single legacy --force-bypass test
1 new ledger event_type 'install_diff_applied' visible in events.jsonl after diff-mode run
snapshotTree + runInit duplication count: was 2 (install + uninstall), now 0 (single source in helpers/init-test-utils.ts)
unit: classifyFreshPath returns 'present-canonical' when path matches expected canonical state
classifyFreshPath returns 'drifted' when path content byte-differs from expected canonical
classifyFreshPath returns 'user-modified' when path is a non-canonical file at managed location (e.g. .fabric as a regular file)
formatDiffFileState returns human-readable string for each enum variant
integration: packages/cli/__tests__/integration/init-guard.test.ts (I2 inverted + new drift-abort test + I3 repurposed)

risks.description: Coverage threshold (70/70) regression on new diff-mode source code if classifier branches aren't fully exercised by the 5-scenario test file
risks.mitigation: New install-diff-mode.test.ts explicitly covers all 4 DiffFileState branches × 5 scenario combinations; add per-branch unit tests for classifyFreshPath in a dedicated install-diff-classify.test.ts if coverage flags lines<70 in install.ts
risks.fallback: Lower coverage threshold to 65/65 for rc.14 release-blocking only AS LAST RESORT — push back up in rc.15 via dedicated unit tests

risks.description: Lock-check expansion to default install breaks tests that run `fab install` in a tmpdir without explicit `fab serve` teardown (lock file lingers from previous test)
risks.mitigation: Ensure all integration tests use createWerewolfFixtureRoot which clones a clean tmpdir per-test; afterEach cleanup removes lock file via cleanupFixtureRoot; if lock-check assertion breaks in any test, scope the lock-check gate to skip when env var FAB_TEST_MODE=1 is set
risks.fallback: Defer lock-check expansion to rc.15; keep current --reapply-only lock-check semantics and document as a known limitation in CHANGELOG

risks.description: agents.meta.json structural compare misclassifies legitimate post-scan state as 'drifted' because runInitScan mutates the file immediately after install writes the empty initial
risks.mitigation: Structural compare rule for agents.meta.json: parse as AgentsMeta, check (a) schema_version field matches expected, (b) revision starts with 'sha256:' prefix, (c) nodes/counters keys exist with expected types. Do NOT byte-compare. Run classifier BEFORE runInitScan in executeInitFabricPlan (snapshot pre-scan state for diff classification, scan mutation happens after).
risks.fallback: Treat any well-formed AgentsMeta as canonical (option 4 from clarification_needs); only abort on parse failure or schema_version mismatch

risks.description: Existing init-guard.test.ts I2/I3 rewrites accidentally drop coverage of the throw-on-existing legacy path that --force still needs
risks.mitigation: Preserve at least one explicit it() block asserting `--force` still overwrites + emits deprecation warning; preserve one explicit it() block asserting `--reapply` still emits reapply_completed ledger event
risks.fallback: Document the legacy paths in CHANGELOG rc.14 section + add TODO comment in install.ts pointing to rc.15 removal milestone

interfaces.name: DiffFileState
interfaces.purpose: Per-file state classification for diff-mode; separate from InitWriteAction to avoid breaking formatInitPathAction switch

interfaces.name: ClassifiedFreshPathResult
interfaces.definition: interface ClassifiedFreshPathResult { path: string; state: DiffFileState; expectedAction: 'write' | 'skip' | 'abort'; reason?: string }
interfaces.purpose: Output of classifyFreshPath — pairs DiffFileState with the action executeInitExecutionPlan should take downstream

key_functions.signature: classifyFreshPath(path: string, opts: { expectedCanonical: Buffer | (() => Buffer); detectStrategy: 'byte' | 'structural' | 'presence' | 'always-rewrite' }): ClassifiedFreshPathResult
key_functions.purpose: Non-throwing replacement for planFreshPath. Inspects path on disk and classifies into DiffFileState based on detectStrategy.
Human-readable label for diff table rendering (mirrors formatInitPathAction L1608).

key_functions.signature: appendInstallDiffLedgerEvent(projectRoot: string, payload: { applied: string[]; canonical: string[]; drifted: string[] }): void
key_functions.purpose: Append install_diff_applied event to .fabric/events.jsonl (mirrors appendReapplyLedgerEvent L1326).
